title: Chronic disease annual review

examination.text: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.
examination.rationale: The exact priorities section supports patient goals, preferences, quality of life, coordination, and agreed priorities without generating disease-specific management.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.

source.title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.8 — treatment burden, day-to-day life, carers, goals, values, and priorities

medication.text: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.
medication.rationale: The exact multimorbidity section supports clinician review of conditions, treatment burden, function, frailty, medicines, and impact without assuming which chronic diseases are present.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.

source.title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.4 — multimorbidity, daily activities, falls, medicines, and frailty context

medication.text: Supports clinician-led review of benefits, harms, treatment burden, personal priorities, and a documented discussion about reducing or stopping treatment without generating a medication change.
medication.rationale: The exact treatment-review section supports recording reviewed results, medicine burden, clinician-entered plan, communication, and follow-up without changing treatment automatically.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led review of benefits, harms, treatment burden, personal priorities, and a documented discussion about reducing or stopping treatment without generating a medication change.

source.title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations

sources_used.title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE geriatric and primary-care adaptation

source_limitations: Specific diagnoses, disease control, observations, tests, targets, medicines, adverse effects, screening, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
